issue.message: Missing permissions required by Vibrator.vibrate: android.permission.VIBRATE
Missing permissions required by Vibrator.vibrate: android.permission.VIBRATE
Missing permissions required by Vibrator.vibrate: android.permission.VIBRATE
Missing permissions required by Vibrator.vibrate: android.permission.VIBRATE
The Google Play store has a policy that limits usage of MANAGE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Field requires API level 30 (current min is 26): `android.view.HapticFeedbackConstants#CONFIRM`
Field requires API level 30 (current min is 26): `android.view.HapticFeedbackConstants#REJECT`
Do not hardcode "/sdcard/"; use `Environment.getExternalStorageDirectory().getPath()` instead
Do not hardcode "/sdcard/"; use `Environment.getExternalStorageDirectory().getPath()` instead
Do not hardcode "/sdcard/"; use `Environment.getExternalStorageDirectory().getPath()` instead

Do not hardcode "/sdcard/"; use `Environment.getExternalStorageDirectory().getPath()` instead
Do not hardcode "`/data/`"; use `Context.getFilesDir().getPath()` instead
Do not hardcode "/sdcard/"; use `Environment.getExternalStorageDirectory().getPath()` instead
Do not hardcode "/sdcard/"; use `Environment.getExternalStorageDirectory().getPath()` instead
TileService#startActivityAndCollapse(Intent) is deprecated. Use TileService#startActivityAndCollapse(PendingIntent) instead.
Suspicious indentation: This is indented but is not continuing the previous expression (`gestureFeedback = Ge`...)
Attribute `enableOnBackInvokedCallback` is only used in API level 33 and higher (current min is 26)

Use of `REQUEST_IGNORE_BATTERY_OPTIMIZATIONS` violates the Play Store Content Policy regarding acceptable use cases, as described in https://developer.android.com/training/monitoring-device-state/doze-standby.html

Use of `REQUEST_IGNORE_BATTERY_OPTIMIZATIONS` violates the Play Store Content Policy regarding acceptable use cases, as described in https://developer.android.com/training/monitoring-device-state/doze-standby.html
Redundant label can be removed
A newer version of androidx.core:core-ktx than 1.13.1 is available: 1.19.0
A newer version of com.google.android.material:material than 1.12.0 is available: 1.14.0
A newer version of androidx.activity:activity-compose than 1.9.3 is available: 1.13.0
A newer version of androidx.navigation:navigation-compose than 2.8.5 is available: 2.9.8

A newer version of androidx.test:runner than 1.6.2 is available: 1.7.0
A newer version of org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-coroutines-android than 1.8.1 is available: 1.11.0

A newer version of org.commonmark:commonmark than 0.21.0 is available: 0.29.0

A newer version of org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-coroutines-test than 1.8.1 is available: 1.11.0
A newer version of org.mockito:mockito-core than 5.12.0 is available: 5.23.0
A newer version of org.mockito.kotlin:mockito-kotlin than 5.4.0 is available: 6.3.0
A newer version of org.json:json than 20231013 is available: 20260522
The native library `arm64-v8a/libtermux.so` (from `com.github.termux.termux-app:terminal-emulator:v0.118.3`) is not 16 KB aligned
The native library `arm64-v8a/libtermux.so` (from `com.github.termux.termux-app:terminal-emulator:v0.118.3`) is not 16 KB aligned
The native library `arm64-v8a/libtermux.so` (from `com.github.termux.termux-app:terminal-emulator:v0.118.3`) is not 16 KB aligned
Setting file permissions to world-readable can be risky, review carefully

The attribute `android:allowBackup` is deprecated from Android 12 and higher and may be removed in future versions. Consider adding the attribute `android:dataExtractionRules` specifying an `@xml` resource which configures cloud backups and device transfers on Android 12 and higher.

Provide a timeout when requesting a wakelock with `PowerManager.Wakelock.acquire(long timeout)`. This will ensure the OS will cleanup any wakelocks that last longer than you intend, and will save your user's battery.
Unnecessary; `SDK_INT` is always >= 26
Unnecessary; `SDK_INT` is always >= 26
Unnecessary; `SDK_INT` is always >= 26
Unnecessary; `SDK_INT` is never < 26
issue.errorLine1: val batteryOk = if (Build.VERSION.SDK_INT < 23) true else (activity.getSystemService(Context.POWER_SERVICE) as? PowerManager)?.isIgnoringBatteryOptimizations(activity.packageName) ?: false

issue.message: Unnecessary; `SDK_INT` is always >= 26
Unnecessary; `SDK_INT` is always >= 26

Unnecessary; `SDK_INT` is always >= 26
Unnecessary; `SDK_INT` is always >= 26
This folder configuration (`v26`) is unnecessary; `minSdkVersion` is 26. Merge all the resources in this folder into `mipmap-anydpi`.
Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `BuildBridgeService` which has field `appCtx` pointing to `Context`); this is a memory leak
Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `CrashReportBridgeService` which has field `appCtx` pointing to `Context`); this is a memory leak
Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `DeployBridgeService` which has field `appCtx` pointing to `Context`); this is a memory leak
Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `NotifyBridgeService` which has field `appCtx` pointing to `Context`); this is a memory leak
Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `ShizukuBridgeService` which has field `appCtx` pointing to `Context`); this is a memory leak

Do not concatenate text displayed with `setText`. Use resource string with placeholders.
String literal in `setText` can not be translated. Use Android resources instead.
